auth.scheme: DD-API-KEY header on every request; metric submission needs an API key only — no DD-APPLICATION-KEY, and the key never goes in the URL or query string
auth.redaction: the API key must never appear in reports, exception text, repr, or logs

common_headers.content_encoding: optional; documented values are deflate, gzip, zstd1 — this client implements deflate (zlib) and gzip and must reject anything else, including zstd1, with a clear error

sites.note: each Datadog site is independent; the API host is https://api.<site>

series_schema.payload: {"series": [Series, ...]} — series is the only top-level key
series_schema.interval: int64 seconds; documented as needed when type is rate or count
series_schema.points: array of {timestamp: int64 POSIX seconds, value: double} objects (v2 uses objects, not the v1 [ts, value] pairs)
series_schema.point_freshness: timestamps must not be more than 1 hour in the past (3600s) or more than 10 minutes in the future (600s); enforce client-side before any POST
series_schema.resources: array of {name, type} objects (v2 replaces the v1 host string)
series_schema.omission: unset optional fields are omitted from the JSON, never sent as null

limits.meaning: a payload on the wire is at most 500KB (512000 bytes); a compressed payload must additionally decompress to less than 5MB (5242880 bytes)
limits.batching: split the series list in order into as few payloads as fit: uncompressed mode packs by serialized JSON size <= 512000; compressed mode packs by decompressed size < 5242880 while the compressed bytes stay <= 512000; a single series that cannot fit is a ValueError

success.note: a 202 whose errors array is non-empty is recorded as a batch with errors and does not count toward accepted

errors.partial_failure: each payload is reported independently; a failed payload must not prevent later payloads from being submitted

rate_limit.policy: sleep exactly Retry-After seconds through the injected sleep and retry the same payload up to max_retries times, then record the 429 as that batch's outcome
rate_limit.note: Datadog does not rate limit metric point submission itself, but 429 is a documented response and the headers family is X-RateLimit-*

report_shape.accepted: series in batches that got 202 with an empty errors array
report_shape.ok: true only if every batch got a clean 202
report_shape.batches: [{status, series_count, errors, attempts}] in submission order
